rationale: EV-001 shows reg.exe executed at High integrity level by host-01\user-01 from C:\Users\IT01-user-01\Downloads\ — an atypical working directory for administrative registry changes — with the explicit command to set HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\EventLog\Start to 4 (disabled), confirmed written by A0. Disabling the Windows Event Log service is a well-known defense-evasion technique used to suppress audit trails. The parent process is cmd.exe with no further command-line context visible, leaving the initiating chain unexplained. No evidence of the EventLog service actually stopping is present in this package, and no broader attack chain is confirmed, but the combination of elevated privileges, suspicious working directory, and deliberate log-suppression registry modification warrants escalation.
